methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
DefSecIntel is a Series A frontier-tech company in the Defense & Security sector. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), with direct peer benchmarking against three same-sector, same-stage companies: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The three comparable companies yield a median valuation of $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M. This method directly anchors the analysis to observable market pricing for companies at identical stage and sector, minimizing methodological variance. The peer set reflects recent private market activity in defense and security infrastructure, where Series A valuations have consolidated in this band.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered at $300M. This narrow range reflects the single applicable methodology; confidence in the median estimate would strengthen with additional methods (revenue multiples, DCF, or late-stage funding signals). Key uncertainties include DefSecIntel's revenue profile relative to peers, customer concentration, and technology differentiation within the Defense & Security subsector. The $150M–$500M peer spread itself indicates meaningful valuation variance driven by factors not captured in stage and sector alone.
